title: PFAS Drinking Water MCLs
subtitle: Maximum contaminant levels under the Safe Drinking Water Act
summary: The Biden EPA finalized MCLs for six PFAS compounds in April 2024. In May 2025, the Trump EPA announced it would retain the 4 ppt MCLs for PFOA and PFOS but rescind the limits for GenX, PFHxS, PFNA, and PFBS — and extend the PFOA/PFOS compliance deadline from 2029 to 2031.
notes: TODO: Update when proposed rule is published in Federal Register. Note expected final rule Spring 2026.

title: PFAS CERCLA Hazardous Substance Designation
subtitle: Superfund designation for PFOA and PFOS
summary: The Biden EPA designated PFOA and PFOS as CERCLA hazardous substances in May 2024, effective July 2024. The Trump EPA announced it would retain this designation and defend it in court, while developing a new Framework Rule to address passive receiver liability for water utilities, municipalities, and farms.
notes: TODO: Update when Framework Rule is proposed. Watch for enforcement discretion policy changes.

title: TSCA Section 8(a)(7) PFAS Reporting Rule
subtitle: Mandatory reporting of PFAS manufacture, processing, and use
summary: The Biden EPA finalized a rule requiring companies to report PFAS manufacture, processing, and use under TSCA Section 8(a)(7). In November 2025, the Trump EPA proposed exemptions — including a de minimis exemption (below 0.1%), an imported articles exemption, and expanded byproduct/impurity exemptions — reducing industry reporting burden by an estimated 10-11 million hours.
notes: TODO: Track comment period (closed December 29, 2025) and final rule date. Note estimated $786-843M savings cited by EPA.
